name: servo
description: SG90/MG90S/MG996R servo motor — position control via 50Hz PWM. 0°–180° range with ~0.5ms–2.5ms pulse width.

parts.name: External power
parts.spec: 5V 1A+ for MG996R (ESP32 3.3V pin cannot power servos!)

wiring.servo_orange_yellow_white: ESP32 GPIO (PWM signal only, low current)
wiring.diagram:
External 5V → Servo Red (+)
ESP32 GND → Servo Brown (-) AND External GND
ESP32 GPIO(D) → Servo Orange/Yellow (Signal)

esp32_constraints.power: CRITICAL: Do NOT power servo from ESP32 3.3V or 5V pin. Use external 5V supply with common ground.
esp32_constraints.gpio_amps: Servo signal pin draws <1mA — any GPIO works.
esp32_constraints.pwm_required: Any output-capable GPIO. ESP32 has 16 PWM channels.
esp32_constraints.signal_voltage: ESP32 GPIO outputs 3.3V. Most servos accept 3.3V logic. If unreliable, use level shifter to 5V.

code.example:
from machine import Pin, PWM
import time

# Setup
servo = PWM(Pin(15), freq=50)  # GPIO 15, 50Hz

def set_angle(angle):
    """Move servo to angle 0-180 degrees."""
    # Map 0-180° to 0.5ms-2.5ms pulse → 26-128 duty at 50Hz/10-bit
    min_duty = 26   # 0.5ms
    max_duty = 128  # 2.5ms
    duty = int(min_duty + (angle / 180.0) * (max_duty - min_duty))
    servo.duty(duty)

# Sweep demo
for angle in range(0, 181, 10):
    set_angle(angle)
    time.sleep_ms(100)

for angle in range(180, -1, -10):
    set_angle(angle)
    time.sleep_ms(100)

# Park at center
set_angle(90)
time.sleep(0.5)
servo.deinit()  # release PWM

pitfalls: POWERING servo from ESP32 pin — will cause brownout/reset. Always use external 5V.
Forgetting common GND between ESP32 and external supply — servo won't move.
freq must be exactly 50. Wrong frequency = servo jitter or no movement.
duty() values out of 26-128 range may cause servo to push against mechanical stops — buzzing, overheating.
duty() accepts 0-1023 at 10-bit resolution. At 50Hz, useful range is ~26-128.
MG996R needs 1-2A — a phone charger USB port won't suffice. Use a dedicated power supply.
PWM.deinit() after use — otherwise servo may twitch on next boot/reset.
